channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Harris says the killing of Sinwar is an opportunity 'to finally end the war in Gaza'
item.description: MILWAUKEE (AP) — Democratic presidential nominee Kamala Harris said Thursday the killing of Hamas' top leader, Yahya Sinwar, by Israel “gives us an opportunity to finally end the war in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN and COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: New renderings show how A's ballpark would transform Las Vegas Strip
item.description: Extensive details have been revealed about the proposed $1.5 billion "multi-purpose, enclosed ballpark" on the Las Vegas Strip that could become home to the Las Vegas Athletics MLB franchise in time for the 2028 baseball season.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, KRON4

item.title: Mitzi Gaynor, ‘South Pacific’ star, dies at 93
item.description: Mitzi Gaynor, the effervescent dancer and actor who starred as Nellie Forbush in the 1958 film "South Pacific" and appeared in other musicals with Bing Crosby, Frank Sinatra and Gene Kelly, has died. She was 93.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Israel's foreign minister confirms that Hamas' top leader Yahya Sinwar was killed in Gaza
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Israel’s foreign minister has confirmed that Israeli troops in Gaza have killed Hamas’ top leader Yahya Sinwar, a chief architect of last year's attack on Israel that sparked the war.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA, SAMY MAGDY and JACK JEFFERY, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Liam Payne, former One Direction member, dies at 31 in Argentina hotel fall
item.description: BUENOS AIRES, Argentina (AP) — Former One Direction singer Liam Payne, 31, whose chart-topping British boy band generated a global following of swooning fans, was found dead Wednesday after falling from a hotel balcony in Buenos Aires, local officials said.
item.dc:creator: ISABEL DEBRE and KAITLYN HUAMANI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Instagram rolls out new features to prevent sextortion scams targeting teens
item.description: Sextortion occurs when a victim is coerced to provide sexually explicit photos or videos of themselves and threatened with them being shared publicly.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, NewsNation

item.title: McConnell privately slammed Trump as ‘stupid’ and ‘despicable’ after 2020 election
item.description: McConnell also criticized the former president as a “narcissist” and admitted after the 2020 election that “it’s not just the Democrats who are counting the days” until he left office.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: GOP cringes over Trump’s vow to wield military against opponents
item.description: Former President Trump’s incendiary comments about deploying the military and using other powers of the executive branch to pursue his political enemies, including a sitting member of Congress, is putting downballot Republican candidates on the defensive.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: 5 takeaways from the last full fundraising quarter before Election Day
item.description: Federal candidates have submitted their reports for what was the final full fundraising quarter before Election Day, offering a snapshot of their financial status with just three weeks to go.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, NewsNation

item.title: Electoral battleground North Carolina starts early in-person voting while recovering from Helene
item.description: ASHEVILLE, N.C. (AP) — Early in-person voting began statewide Thursday in the presidential battleground of North Carolina, including in mountainous areas where thousands of potential voters still lack power and clean running water after Hurricane Helene's epic flooding.
item.dc:creator: MAKIYA SEMINERA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Universal Orlando announces Epic Universe opening date
item.description: The theme park will feature more than 50 attractions, as well as entertainment, dining and shopping options across five “expansive worlds.
item.dc:creator: Scott Gustin, Fox 5

item.title: McConnell privately slammed Trump as ‘stupid’ and ‘despicable’ after 2020 election
item.description: Senate Republican Leader Mitch McConnell (Ky.) privately slammed former President Donald Trump as “stupid as well as being ill-tempered” and “a despicable human being” after the 2020 election, according to a biography of the senator set to be released later this month.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, ABC 4

item.title: After hurricane, with no running water, residents organize to meet a basic need
item.description: It takes water to flush a toilet yet tens of thousands of North Carolinians have been without it since Hurricane Helene ripped through the state three weeks ago.
item.dc:creator: Brittany Peterson, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Instagram rolls out new features to prevent sextortion scams targeting teens
item.description: Instagram has introduced a series of new safety features to prevent sextortion schemes, marking the platform's latest effort to protect users, especially teenagers, from online harm.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, Fox 5

item.title: The Biden administration has now canceled loans for more than 1 million public workers
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A student loan cancellation program for public workers has granted relief to more than 1 million Americans — up from just 7,000 who were approved before it was updated by the Biden administration two years ago.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Asian American evangelicals' theology is conservative. But that doesn't mean they vote that way
item.description: The Rev. Wayne Lee leads an English-speaking church of second- and third-generation Chinese Americans in the heart of Philadelphia’s Chinatown.
item.dc:creator: DEEPA BHARATH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Uphill battles that put abortion rights on ballots are unlikely to end even if the measures pass
item.description: Voters in nine states are deciding next month whether to add the right to abortion to their constitutions.
item.dc:creator: Geoff Mulvvihill, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: The ballot’s mailed but you die before Election Day. Now what?
item.description: Some states allow them to be counted, and some don't. But most states have never addressed the question.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: Dollar General employee finds puppies dumped in trash amid cold Arkansas weather
item.description: Three puppies, less than a week old, were abandoned this week inside of a Dollar General Market dumpster, according to Arkansas authorities.
item.dc:creator: Elena Ramirez, KRON4

item.title: A Texas board rejects clemency plea from a man facing execution in shaken baby syndrome case
item.description: HOUSTON (AP) — A Texas state board on Wednesday declined to stop what could be the first execution in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: JUAN A. LOZANO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Family of Erik and Lyle Menendez call for their release and say they're victims who were vilified
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Multiple generations of family members of Erik and Lyle Menendez pleaded for the brothers' release from prison on Wednesday, saying they deserve to be free despite life sentences for the 1989 killings of their parents in Beverly Hills because they had been “brutalized” and sexuall
item.dc:creator: STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: La Nina could soon arrive. Here's what that means for winter weather
item.description: NOAA’s Climate Prediction Center reports there is a 60% chance that a weak La Nina will develop this autumn and could last until March.
item.dc:creator: Isabella O'Malley, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: What to know about shaken baby syndrome as a Texas man could be first in US executed over it
item.description: HOUSTON (AP) — A Texas man this week could become the first person executed in the U.S. for a murder conviction tied to the diagnosis of shaken baby syndrome.
item.dc:creator: JUAN A. LOZANO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Nebraska court allows voter registrations from people with felony convictions
item.description: The Nebraska Supreme Court rejected the contention that the state’s pardon board has sole authority to restore such rights.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: FTC announces rule to make it easier to cancel subscriptions
item.description: The department will soon implement a "final click to cancel rule," a move that will require sellers to make it "as easy for consumers to cancel their enrollment as it was to sign up.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, NewsNation

item.title: Witnesses saw an armed group harassing Helene aid workers in a small Tennessee town, sheriff says
item.description: NASHVILLE, Tenn. (AP) — Witnesses reported seeing a group of armed people harassing hurricane relief workers in a remote Tennessee community last weekend, a sheriff said Wednesday as a man in North Carolina appeared in court for allegedly threatening aid workers in that state.
item.dc:creator: KIMBERLEE KRUESI and SARAH BRUMFIELD, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Man arrested near Trump Coachella rally with gun sues for defamation
item.description: The man arrested near former President Trump’s Coachella, Calif., rally over the weekend sued the Riverside County sheriff for defamation and other claims, saying there were “preposterous allegations” made about the incident possibly being an assassination attempt.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Brightest supermoon rising this week, and it won't be alone
item.description: Get ready to look up for the year’s biggest and brightest supermoon. And it's pairing up with a comet for a rare stargazing two-for-one.
item.dc:creator: Dominique Jack, KRON4

item.title: Former Vegas Democrat politician convicted of killing reporter is sentenced to at least 28 years
item.description: Robert Telles, a former Las Vegas-area Democratic politician convicted of killing a journalist, faces at least 28 years in prison before he becomes eligible for parole.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hurricane fallout threatens to hinder voting
item.description: The fallout from a pair of deadly hurricanes threatens to roil voting in the Southeast with complications for election officials and residents alike in states key to this year’s presidential race.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Mexico's former public security chief set to be sentenced in US drug case
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Mexico's former public security chief is set to be sentenced in a U.S. court on Wednesday after being convicted of taking bribes to aid drug traffickers.
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: North Carolina man shot, killed outside of his own wedding; family wants answers
item.description: Tyrek Burton, 37, was fatally shot outside his own wedding reception at Barber Park Events Center in Greensboro, North Carolina, over the weekend.
item.dc:creator: Elijah Skipper, KRON4

item.title: Man to row across the Pacific Ocean to honor victims of war in Ukraine
item.description: Aurimas Mockus from Lithuania has embarked on a historic journey across the Pacific Ocean from San Diego to Australia in a solo rowing boat, aiming to be the first person in the world to complete the 12,000 kilometer trip alone, without help or stops.
item.dc:creator: Dan Plante, WGN 9

item.title: Long lost sisters meet for the first time in person
item.description: Two long-lost sisters who met for the first time in person say their love will continue even after this life.
item.dc:creator: Alina Lee, Fox 5

item.title: Rufus Wainwright blasts Trump playing 'Hallelujah' at town hall: 'The height of blasphemy'
item.description: The 51-year-old singer said Tuesday that he was "horrified" to learn his 2001 cover of Leonard Cohen's "Hallelujah" was part of the playlist at the Trump campaign event a night earlier.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, Fox 5

item.title: Trump rally turns into awkward dance party
item.description: What began as a campaign town hall in the Philadelphia suburbs quickly turned into an impromptu dance party Monday night after Donald Trump was twice interrupted by medical emergencies in the audience.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, KTLA 5

item.title: Jury selection underway in the trial of a man accused of killing 2 teen girls in 2017
item.description: INDIANAPOLIS (AP) — Eight women and six men have been selected so far to a jury in the trial of a man charged in the Indiana killings of two teenage girls during a winter hike in 2017.
item.dc:creator: RICK CALLAHAN, Associated Press, ABC 4
